sql_array: CREATE TABLE users (id INT PRIMARY KEY, username VARCHAR(50), password_hash VARCHAR(40), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO users (id, username, password_hash) VALUES (1, 'testUser', bif:sha1('password123'));
CREATE TABLE resources (id INT PRIMARY KEY, owner_id INT, resource_type VARCHAR(20), CONSTRAINT fk_owner FOREIGN KEY (owner_id) REFERENCES users(id));
INSERT INTO resources (id, owner_id, resource_type) VALUES (1, 1, 'document'), (2, 1, 'image');
SELECT USER_DROP(1) AS result FROM DUAL WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM resources WHERE owner_id = 1) > 0;
SELECT bif:sha1(username) AS hashed_username, COUNT(*) FROM users GROUP BY username HAVING hashed_username IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE temp_users (user_id INT, username VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE);
INSERT INTO temp_users (user_id, username) VALUES (NULL, NULL), (2, 'anotherUser');
SELECT bif:sha1('user' || user_id) AS user_hash FROM temp_users WHERE user_id IS NOT NULL;
SELECT u.username AS user, u.id AS userId, r.resource_type FROM users u JOIN resources r ON u.id = r.owner_id WHERE bif:sha1(u.username) = 'dummyhash' OR r.resource_type IS NULL;
SELECT bif:sha1(NULLIF(username, 'invalid_user')) FROM users WHERE created_at < CURRENT_TIMESTAMP AND username IS NOT NULL;
SELECT bif:sha1(username), COUNT(*) FROM users GROUP BY username HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
